symptom.description: P0087 DTC active, MIL on, low power under load. Common rail fuel pressure reported by FRP sensor PID is below PCM threshold. Vehicle: 2018 Ford F-250 6.7L Power Stroke Diesel. No prior test results at intake.

components.body: The HP pump itself is mechanical: gear-driven off the camshaft, internal cam profile drives two main pistons. The fuel volume control valve sits on top and is what regulates how much fuel actually gets pressurized — that's the electrical lever PCM has on this pump. Checked with mechanical pressure tests, not by probing wires.
components.name: Bosch CP4.2 High-Pressure Fuel Pump
components.role: Generates the rail pressure that feeds the injectors. Driven mechanically — no electrical input of its own.
components.function: Raises fuel pressure from low-pressure supply to common rail operating pressure (up to ~29,000 PSI peak); internally lubricated entirely by diesel fuel with no separate oil circuit
components.location: front of engine, gear-driven off front cover area
components.subtitle: Gear-Driven from the Camshaft
components.wire_summary: None — the volume control valve mounted on top is the only electrical interface

components.body: Two-wire electric pump in the tank. PCM commands it on. There is typically a fuel pump driver module or a relay sitting upstream between PCM and the pump's power wire — those are conditional branches that only get tested when probing shows the power wire isn't getting 12V on command.
components.name: Electric Lift Pump
components.role: Pulls fuel through the tank-mounted 10-micron filter/water separator and pushes it forward to the engine-mounted 4-micron filter, then on to the high-pressure pump. Target supply pressure: roughly 55 psi at idle.
components.function: Moves fuel from tank at low pressure forward to the filter/water separator and CP4.2 inlet
components.location: driver-side frame rail
components.subtitle: Low-Pressure Electric Lift Pump
components.electrical_contract: electric motor; voltage, current draw, and connector type not yet captured

components.body: Works in tandem with the volume control valve on the HP pump. Volume control sets how much fuel enters the high-pressure side; this regulator sets how much returns. Together they hold the rail at the target pressure PCM commands.
components.name: FRP Regulator
components.role: PCM-modulated solenoid that trims return flow out of the rail. Sets the downstream side of rail pressure.
components.subtitle: Fuel Rail Pressure Control Valve
components.wire_summary: 2 wires, both go to PCM, PWM-modulated
components.probing_tactic: Back-probe either pin while engine running. PWM should be present and modulate with commanded pressure.

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 1)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 1; exact cylinder-to-bank mapping not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; voltage, current, and connector details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 2)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 2; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 3)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 3; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 4)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 4; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 5)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 5; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 6)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 6; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 7)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 7; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Injector (Cylinder 8)
components.function: Solenoid-operated common rail injector; injects metered high-pressure fuel into combustion chamber on PCM command
components.location: cylinder 8; exact position not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: high-voltage/high-current PWM via dedicated PCM driver circuit; details not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Level Sender
components.function: Reads fuel level via float-and-resistor mechanism; outputs varying resistance signal for fuel gauge
components.location: inside the fuel tank
components.electrical_contract: resistive analog (variable resistance); wire count and resistance range not yet captured

components.name: Fuel Rail Mechanical Pressure Relief Valve
components.function: Passive mechanical overpressure protection; opens at factory-set cracking pressure to return fuel and prevent rail overpressure damage
components.location: on the high-pressure fuel rail(s); whether one per rail or one total not yet captured

components.body: 3-wire sensor at the front of the driver-side fuel rail. Signal wire goes direct to PCM. The 5V reference and low reference each route through shared splices with other sensors on the way to/from PCM. Which other sensors share those splices is a label that gets filled in over time — but the SHARING is canonical. A failed shared 5V or low-ref splice will pull down every sensor on that bus.
components.name: Fuel Rail Pressure (FRP) Sensor
components.role: Reports actual rail pressure to PCM so the PWM control loop can hit its target.
components.function: Measures common rail fuel pressure and provides analog voltage feedback to the PCM for closed-loop IMV control
components.location: on the high-pressure fuel rail(s); exact number (one total or one per rail) not yet captured
components.subtitle: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor
components.electrical_contract: 3-wire analog: 5V reference, analog voltage signal, ground

components.name: Fuel Tank
components.function: Stores diesel fuel; single tank, 26–48 gallons depending on cab/bed configuration; no factory dual-tank option on this generation
components.location: under the cab/frame

components.name: High-Pressure Fuel Rail (Bank A)
components.function: Stores and distributes high-pressure fuel to four injectors on Bank A; acts as hydraulic accumulator to damp pressure fluctuations
components.location: one cylinder bank; per-bank side (driver/passenger) not yet captured

components.body: The passenger-side rail has no electrical components in this fuel system. All electrical diagnostic on the rails happens on the driver-side rail (FRP sensor at the front, FRP regulator at the back).
components.name: High-Pressure Fuel Rail (Bank B)
components.role: Distributes fuel to the passenger-side injectors.
components.function: Stores and distributes high-pressure fuel to four injectors on Bank B; acts as hydraulic accumulator to damp pressure fluctuations
components.location: opposite cylinder bank from Rail A; per-bank side not yet captured
components.subtitle: Mechanical fuel routing only
components.wire_summary: None — no electrical components on this rail in this system

components.body: PCM controls the milliamps through this coil by modulating duty cycle. Whether one side is switched 12V and the other is PCM-modulated ground (or both wires connect directly to PCM internal drivers) varies by design — for diagnostic purposes both wires should show PCM activity when commanded.
components.name: Inlet Metering Valve (IMV)
components.role: PCM-modulated solenoid that meters fuel into the high-pressure pumping chambers. Sets the upstream side of rail pressure (the FRP regulator sets the downstream side).
components.function: Normally-open PWM-controlled solenoid that meters fuel quantity entering the CP4.2 high-pressure circuit under PCM closed-loop control
components.location: inside the CP4.2 pump body
components.subtitle: Metering Unit · on the high-pressure pump
components.wire_summary: 2 wires, both go to PCM, PWM-modulated
components.probing_tactic: Back-probe either pin while engine is running. PWM signal should be present and change with load and commanded rail pressure.
components.electrical_contract: PWM solenoid; normally open; wire count, PWM frequency, duty-cycle range, and coil resistance not yet captured

components.name: Lift Pump Relay
components.function: Switches battery power to the electric lift pump on PCM command
components.location: Battery Junction Box (BJB) or Smart Junction Box (SJB) in engine compartment; exact box and cavity not yet captured
components.electrical_contract: standard automotive relay (coil + contacts); cavity designation, coil resistance, and contact rating not yet captured

components.name: Passive Low-Pressure Fuel Return Circuit
components.function: Passive (no return pump) return of excess high-pressure fuel from injectors and pressure relief valve to the fuel circuit; termination point (tank vs. filter housing) not yet captured
components.location: routes from injectors and/or pressure relief valve back to fuel tank or filter housing; exact routing not yet captured

components.body: PCM drives the lift pump command, modulates the two PWM regulators (volume control valve on the HP pump, and the FRP pressure regulator at the back of the rail), supplies 5V reference to the sensors, and reads the FRP sensor signal. PCM modulates duty cycle on regulator drivers — duty cycle commanded determines milliamps through each coil.
components.name: Powertrain Control Module (PCM)
components.role: Controller — every electrical wire in this system terminates here
components.function: Sole fuel system controller; commands IMV, injectors, and lift pump relay; performs closed-loop rail pressure control via FRP feedback; broadcasts fuel system PIDs on HS-CAN at 500 kbps
components.location: passenger-side firewall, engine compartment
components.subtitle: Powertrain Control Module
components.unknown_note: PCM connector IDs and cavity numbers for each wire — not yet captured.
components.wire_summary: 9 wires routed into this system (4 of which go to two PWM regulators, 3 go to the FRP sensor, 2 go to the lift pump)
components.probing_tactic: Probing tactic: PCM connector itself is rarely the failure. Probe at the COMPONENT end first (sensor or regulator side), and only walk back to PCM if the wire between them tests open.
components.electrical_contract: connector designations and pin counts not yet captured

components.name: Primary Fuel Filter and Water Separator Assembly
components.function: Removes particulate contamination and separates free water from diesel fuel before the high-pressure pump; houses the WIF sensor
components.location: driver-side frame rail, downstream of lift pump

components.body: When several sensors on the same vehicle all report "5V REF dead" or similar voltage-reference faults at once, suspect this splice or PCM's 5V supply itself rather than each sensor independently.
components.name: Shared 5V Reference Splice
components.role: Common 5V reference distribution from PCM to multiple sensors
components.subtitle: Multiple sensors share PCM's 5V supply through this junction
components.unknown_note: Which OTHER sensors share this 5V reference on this vehicle — not yet captured (this gets filled in as you build out other sensor topologies in the system).
components.wire_summary: PCM's 5V output enters; multiple sensor 5V REF wires (FRP sensor + others) leave

components.body: Sensor low-reference is a precise return path through PCM's analog ground — NOT chassis ground. When several sensors read biased high at once, suspect this splice or PCM's low-reference circuit.
components.name: Shared Low-Reference Splice
components.role: Common return / "sensor ground" path back to PCM
components.subtitle: Sensor return path back to PCM
components.unknown_note: Which OTHER sensors share this low-reference splice — not yet captured.
components.wire_summary: Multiple sensor LOW REF wires converge; one return wire to PCM

components.name: Water-in-Fuel (WIF) Sensor
components.function: Detects free water accumulation at the filter/separator and signals the PCM to trigger the WIF warning lamp
components.location: integrated into the primary fuel filter housing on the driver-side frame rail
components.electrical_contract: wire count and signal type (analog, discrete, or frequency) not yet captured

pins.name: Volume Control Valve · Pin A
pins.label_gap: Wire color and pin number — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Flat at 12V or flat at 0V → wire broken between PCM and this pin, OR PCM driver issue (rare). Cross-check the other pin (B) to localize.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe pin A at the volume control valve connector on top of the HP pump
pins.expected_reading: PWM activity when engine running. Duty cycle varies with engine load and the rail pressure PCM is commanding.

pins.name: Volume Control Valve · Pin B
pins.label_gap: Wire color and pin number — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Same diagnostic logic as pin A. If A shows PWM and B is dead, the wire to B is the failure.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe pin B at the volume control valve connector
pins.expected_reading: PWM activity when engine running, complementary or inverse of pin A depending on the wiring convention

pins.name: FRP Regulator · Pin A
pins.label_gap: Wire color and pin number — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Flat → wire broken between PCM and this pin, OR PCM driver issue. Cross-check pin B.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe pin A at the FRP regulator connector (back of DS rail)
pins.expected_reading: PWM activity when engine running. Duty cycle varies with rail-pressure target.

pins.name: FRP Regulator · Pin B
pins.label_gap: Wire color and pin number — not yet captured.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe pin B at the FRP regulator connector
pins.expected_reading: PWM activity when engine running, complementary to pin A

pins.name: FRP Sensor · 5V Reference
pins.label_gap: Wire color and sensor pin number — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: 0V → PCM's 5V supply dead, OR the shared 5V splice broken, OR the wire open. If multiple sensors share this 5V (typical), they'll all fail together — check another 5V-using sensor to localize whether it's the shared splice or just this wire.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe the 5V REF pin at the FRP sensor connector

pins.name: FRP Sensor · Low Reference
pins.label_gap: Wire color and sensor pin number — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Reading above 0V → bad return path through the shared splice, OR shared splice open, OR PCM low-ref ground broken. Other sensors on the same low-ref will read biased high at the same time.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe the LOW REF pin at the FRP sensor connector
pins.expected_reading: ~0V — sensor's analog return through PCM via the shared low-ref splice

pins.name: FRP Sensor · Signal
pins.label_gap: Wire color, sensor pin number, and the exact voltage-to-pressure curve for this sensor — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Stuck at 0V → signal shorted to ground, OR sensor failed, OR the 5V reference is dead (check the 5V pin next). Stuck at 5V → signal wire open, OR shorted to 5V. Reads correctly but a pressure DTC is set → sensor is reading reality; the issue is the regulator or volume control valve, not the sensor.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe the signal pin at the FRP sensor connector (front of DS rail)
pins.expected_reading: Analog voltage between 0V and 5V, proportional to rail pressure. Low at rest, climbs as the rail pressurizes. Exact voltage curve for this sensor — not yet captured.

pins.name: Lift Pump · 12V Power
pins.label_gap: Wire color, connector pin number, prime duration, and upstream relay/fuse identifiers — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Reading 0V when commanded → expand the conditional branch and probe upstream: fuel pump relay, fuse, and any fuel pump driver module. Reading 12V but pump silent → ground side broken, OR pump motor seized.
pins.probe_location: Back-probe the power wire at the lift pump connector under the truck near the tank
pins.expected_reading: 12V when commanded by PCM (key-on prime, cranking, or running)

pins.name: Lift Pump · Ground
pins.label_gap: Wire color and the exact chassis stud location — not yet captured.
pins.missing_logic: Open or high resistance → broken ground wire between the pump and the chassis stud, OR corroded ground point.
pins.probe_location: Resistance check from the ground wire at the lift pump connector to chassis ground

connections.description: Low-pressure pressurized fuel from lift pump to filter/water separator inlet
High-pressure fuel from CP4.2 outlet to Bank B fuel rail
PCM commands the lift pump relay coil to switch battery power to the lift pump
PCM commands IMV via high-frequency PWM electrical signal to meter fuel entering CP4.2 high-pressure circuit
High-pressure fuel from Bank A rail to injector 1
PCM drives injector 1 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
FRP regulator is bolted to the back of the driver-side rail (bank-a) — physical mechanical mount that gives the regulator a port to vent rail pressure
High-pressure fuel from CP4.2 outlet to Bank A fuel rail
High-pressure fuel from Bank A rail to injector 2
PCM drives injector 2 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
FRP sensor low-reference return wire runs back to the shared low-ref splice on the way to PCM
PCM low-reference sink receives sensor return path from the shared low-ref splice; splice collects the low-ref wires from FRP sensor and other sensors on the same bus
PCM commands FRP regulator via high-frequency PWM electrical signal to trim fuel return out of the driver-side rail and set downstream rail pressure
Shared 5V splice distributes PCM 5V reference to the FRP sensor 5V REF pin
Relay contact supplies switched battery voltage to the electric lift pump motor
Low-pressure fuel supply from tank to lift pump inlet
High-pressure fuel from Bank B rail to injector 8
PCM drives injector 8 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
High-pressure fuel from Bank B rail to injector 5
PCM drives injector 5 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
Return fuel routed back to the fuel tank OR filter housing (exact termination point not yet captured — GAP); shown here as tank per narration listing tank first
PCM 5V reference output feeds the shared 5V sensor splice; splice distributes 5V to FRP sensor and other sensors on the same bus
Filtered low-pressure fuel from filter housing to CP4.2 inlet
High-pressure fuel from Bank B rail to injector 6
PCM drives injector 6 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
High-pressure fuel from Bank A rail to injector 3
PCM drives injector 3 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
Fuel level sender resistive analog signal routes to PCM (or possibly directly to cluster — routing is a captured GAP; shown here per PATTERN inference I8)
WIF sensor signals water presence in the filter housing to the PCM via an electrical signal (signal type not yet captured)
FRP sensor outputs 3-wire analog signal (5V ref from PCM, analog signal return, ground) to PCM for closed-loop rail pressure control
High-pressure fuel from Bank A rail to injector 4
PCM drives injector 4 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
High-pressure fuel from Bank B rail to injector 7
PCM drives injector 7 via dedicated high-voltage/high-current PWM injector driver circuit
Fuel diverted from rail by pressure relief valve into the passive return circuit when cracking pressure is exceeded
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 1 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 2 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 8 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 5 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 6 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 3 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 4 to passive return circuit
Excess high-pressure fuel return from injector 7 to passive return circuit

scenarios.sub: Vehicle asleep — no power anywhere in this system
scenarios.label: Key Off

scenarios.sub: PCM awake, 5V reference hot, sensors reading rest pressure. Lift pump primes briefly then stops.
scenarios.label: Key On · Engine Off

scenarios.sub: Lift pump steady, both PWM regulators at moderate duty, FRP reading idle rail pressure
scenarios.label: Engine Idle

scenarios.sub: Cruising / light throttle — slight bump in PWM duty above idle
scenarios.label: Light Load

scenarios.sub: Towing or moderate acceleration — higher PWM duty, rising rail pressure
scenarios.label: Medium Load

scenarios.sub: WOT or heavy tow — peak duty cycles, peak rail pressure
scenarios.label: Heavy Load

scenarios.sub: FRP signal stuck high — PCM cutting volume control AND opening regulator to bleed pressure off the rail
scenarios.label: Fault Sim: Pegged High Pressure

scenarios.sub: FRP signal flat low — PCM commanding max volume in AND closing return to try to build pressure
scenarios.label: Fault Sim: No Rail Pressure

pinReadings.reading: 12V steady (pump runs regardless of pressure fault)
12V steady — if pump is actually silent, suspect ground or pump motor
Continuity to chassis (resistance check valid any time)
Continuity to chassis · sinking pump current
PWM · moderate duty (metering fuel for idle rail pressure)
PWM · LOW duty — PCM cutting volume to try to bring pressure down
PWM · MAX duty — PCM trying to build pressure
PWM activity, complementary to pin A
Low voltage representing rest rail pressure
Pegged near 5V — this is what the fault is showing
Flat low even while cranking — this is what the fault is showing
~5V steady (5V supply is not the issue here)
~5V — but verify here first: if 0V, the "no pressure" fault is actually a 5V REF failure, not a rail problem
~0V — verify: if biased high, the FRP signal fault is actually a low-ref splice failure
PWM · moderate duty (trimming return for idle pressure)
PWM · MAX duty — PCM opening return to bleed off rail pressure
PWM · low duty — PCM closing return to hold what pressure exists
PWM activity, complementary to pin A
